view vs update access model

evidence_snippets: Only Update role can initiate TRR/chargeback
Only users with Update can initiate

Only authorized users can log in

once people are authenticated the app shouldn’t bounce them

User Access Changes report for A&R

report includes reinstatement events

evidence_snippets: only System Administrator can reinstate
coverage_entries.reason: Reinstatement limited to system admin asserted multiple times.

evidence_snippets: reinstatement is limited to System Admin
coverage_entries.reason: System Admin is defined as sole reinstatement authority.

coverage_entries.reason: Documents state end users have no direct DB/file access.

evidence_snippets: All updates go through the application.
coverage_entries.reason: Updates must be performed via the application, not directly.

evidence_snippets: Merchant Operating Regulations
coverage_entries.reason: Business must map to and conform with network operating rules.

coverage_entries.reason: Meeting notes require business rules align to those regulations.

evidence_snippets: align to Regulation E or Z
coverage_entries.reason: Compliance requires alignment to Regulation E and Z.

coverage_entries.reason: Business rules must comply with Reg E/Z per notes.

coverage_entries.reason: Sources mandate detailed audit trail for every case touch.

coverage_entries.reason: All modifying actions are recorded in the case history.

coverage_entries.reason: Spec explicitly targets internal and external users.

coverage_entries.reason: Documents require issuer partitioning and guardrails.

coverage_entries.reason: UI supports direct entry and real‑time transaction views.

evidence_snippets: View vs Update. Only Update role can initiate
coverage_entries.reason: Role‑based permissions control initiation and follow‑ups.

coverage_entries.reason: Spec defines view‑only access for authorized users.

evidence_snippets: Only Update role can initiate TRR/chargeback
coverage_entries.reason: Update privilege grants initiation and update capabilities.

evidence_snippets: Only eligible transactions (age window per Reg/network)
coverage_entries.reason: UI must block too‑old transactions and allow dispute initiation.

evidence_snippets: allow combining multiple transactions from the same merchant
coverage_entries.reason: Multi‑transaction cases allowed if same‑merchant constraint met.

evidence_snippets: Guided fields (wizard/stepper) collect required info
coverage_entries.reason: TRR creation is a guided UI and produces a merchant letter.

evidence_snippets: TRR types: original receipt, copy, portfolio
coverage_entries.reason: Three TRR types specified; portfolio defined in notes.

evidence_snippets: external users must NOT request 'original'
coverage_entries.reason: Meeting and transcript state original receipt is internal‑only.

evidence_snippets: show a full confirmation—case number, type, due date
coverage_entries.reason: Notes and emails require confirmation with case, type, due date.

evidence_snippets: Chargeback can be 'pending' or 'immediate.'
send chargeback notification via Letters app
coverage_entries.reason: Chargeback flow, required prompts, and printed notification specified.

evidence_snippets: confirmation: case number, pending/immediate, due date
coverage_entries.reason: Documentation mandates confirmations with case, type, and due date.

evidence_snippets: Zero tolerance—block 100% of duplicates.
coverage_entries.reason: Compliance and notes explicitly require blocking duplicates 100%.

evidence_snippets: Reason codes context‑sensitive by txn type and MCC
coverage_entries.reason: Multiple sources require reason codes derived from type and MCC.

evidence_snippets: open case details with history and follow‑ups

Filters: date range, dispute type, status, reason code

evidence_snippets: color cues paired with labels/icons
color accents paired with a text label
coverage_entries.reason: List view must distinguish type/status with color and labels.

evidence_snippets: full audit trail: user + timestamp on create/modify
view the complete timeline/history on the case
coverage_entries.reason: Audit trail and full case history viewing are repeatedly required.

evidence_snippets: show actions that apply to the case’s current status
Follow‑up actions menu filtered by case status and user access
coverage_entries.reason: Source explicitly requires filtered follow‑up actions

evidence_snippets: who did what and when
each create/modify stamped with user + timestamp

evidence_snippets: system updates ... put a new entry in the history
trigger an in‑app notification for the case creator

evidence_snippets: formatted to Letters app spec -> Print Letter Utility
must match the Print Letter Utility’s format exactly

evidence_snippets: purge dispute cases from the online disputes database
closed cases older than six months must be purged
coverage_entries.reason: Source specifies six‑month online purge of closed cases

unsupported_implied.text: System notifications for system-driven case updates must be delivered only as in‑app alerts on next sign‑in; email notifications must not be used for these notifications.
unsupported_implied.source_snippet: When the system updates a case automatically—aging, deadlines—we’ll put a new entry in the history and show a notification to the case’s creator the next time they sign in. No emails, just an in‑app alert.
unsupported_implied.reason: The gold set requires notification on next logon for system actions but does not prohibit or restrict alternate channels; the source explicitly mandates in‑app‑only notifications and forbids email, which is a distinct constraint on notification delivery not present in the gold requirements.

unsupported_implied.text: The weekly User Access Changes report must include audit fields: timestamps and the actor/admin (who performed the add/change/reinstatement) for new users, role changes, and reinstatements.
unsupported_implied.source_snippet: Weekly “User Access Changes” report for A&R: includes new users and any access/role changes; include reinstatement events; timestamps; actor/admin recorded. [Asha to specify fields]
unsupported_implied.reason: While the gold set requires a User Report of new users and access changes, it does not mandate specific audit fields; the source explicitly requires timestamps and the acting admin/actor be recorded on the weekly report, which is a specific reporting capability not specified in the gold requirements.

unsupported_implied.text: Once authenticated and authorization validated, the application must preserve user session continuity and must not unexpectedly sign users out (session stability behavior aligned to security policy).
unsupported_implied.source_snippet: For sign‑in, once people are authenticated and their permissions are validated, the app shouldn’t randomly bounce them. We’ll follow security policy for session timeouts.
unsupported_implied.reason: The gold requirements mandate authorized login but do not specify session stability or prohibiting unexpected sign‑outs; the source introduces a behavioral requirement that authenticated users should not be unpredictably logged out and that session handling must follow policy, which is a significant operational/session management capability not covered by the gold set.
